data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-24T00:46:46.927Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 7+'s **`ExecuteUpdateAsync`** and **`ExecuteDeleteAsync`** — they translate directly to SQL `UPDATE`/`DELETE` statements, bypassing the change tracker entirely:

```csharp
// Operation 1: single UPDATE statement, no entities loaded
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s =>
        s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: single DELETE statement, no entities loaded
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Key things to know:**

- These run **immediately** — no `SaveChangesAsync()` needed.
- The **DbContext change tracker is not updated**, so any already-tracked entities will be stale. Call `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` afterward if you continue using the same context.
- They participate in an ambient `DbContext` transaction, or you can wrap them in an explicit one.
- Make sure `LastSoldDate` and `CreatedAt` columns are **indexed** for best performance on 500K/2M rows.
data.reasoningText: The user wants to optimize bulk update and bulk delete operations in EF Core 8. These are perfect use cases for `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync`, which were introduced in EF Core 7 and execute directly on the database without loading entities into memory.
